title: Shakshuka

overview.description: Eggs gently poached in a rich tomato and pepper sauce flavoured with cumin, paprika and garlic. A classic North African breakfast that's equally good for brunch or a light dinner.

method: Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over a medium heat.
Cook the onion and pepper for 8–10 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the cumin, smoked paprika and chilli flakes, cooking for 30 seconds.
Add the chopped tomatoes and sugar, if using.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Simmer gently for 15–20 minutes until the sauce has thickened.
Make eight small wells in the sauce.
Crack an egg into each well.
Cover with a lid and cook for 6–8 minutes until the egg whites are set but the yolks remain runny.
Scatter with chopped parsley before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Although now strongly associated with the Middle East, Shakshuka is believed to have originated in North Africa before spreading across the region.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a simple tomato sauce, bloom spices and gently poach eggs directly in the sauce.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A nutritious, inexpensive breakfast that uses pantry staples and introduces North African flavours in an approachable way.
chapman-tips: Reduce the tomato sauce well before adding the eggs.
Use the back of a spoon to make wells for the eggs.
Covering the pan helps the egg whites set evenly.
Frozen crusty bread works well after a quick refresh in the oven.
notes.batch-cooking: The tomato sauce freezes well. Defrost, reheat and add freshly cracked eggs when serving.
notes.leftovers: Leftover sauce makes an excellent pasta sauce or base for baked beans.
